name: Onion Blossoms
recipeInstructions:
1. Leaving the root end intact, carefully peel the outer skin off the onion.
2. Cut a small slice off the top of the onion.
3. Starting at the top of the onion on one side, make a cut downward toward the root end, stopping 1/8 inch from the bottom.
4. Make additional cuts about 1/8 inch from the first cut until there are cuts completely across the top of the onion.
5. Turn the onion 1/4 turn so that the slices are horizontal to you.
6. Repeat steps 3 and 4, making cuts about 1/8 inch apart until it looks like a checkerboard pattern across the top.
7. To make the dip: In a small bowl, combine mayonnaise, sour cream, chili powder, and 1 1/2 teaspoons of Cajun seasoning. Mix well.
8. In a gallon plastic bag, combine flour and the remaining Cajun seasoning.
9. Place milk in a small, deep bowl.
10. Coat the cut onion with flour.
11. Dip the onion in milk, then coat again with the flour mixture.
12. Fry in oil that covers the onion at 350 degrees for 5 minutes, or until golden brown.
13. Place on a serving plate.
14. Discard the very center of the fried onion blossom.
15. Place a few spoonfuls of dip in the center.
16. Serve immediately.
